title: Essential Entryway Shelf with Hooks
summary: A compact wall shelf with angled 45° side brackets, a divided cubby row below, and a double-layered top — sized as shown, but explicitly built to be modified to any length.
description:
This shelf's sides get their shape from a single 45° cut, 4" back and 4" up, giving the whole unit an angled support bracket rather than a plain rectangle — a simple cut that reads as a deliberate design detail rather than just "a board." The divided cubby section below the main shelf comes from notched divider boards that lay flat for cutting, then rotate up into place once notched, an easier sequencing than trying to notch a board that's already standing on edge.

**This plan is explicitly written to be resized** — the source gives the full cut list for a 72-3/4" shelf as shown, but flags exactly which pieces change length and by how much (consistently 2-1/2" less than your desired overall shelf length) if you need a different size for your own entryway. Decide your final length before cutting a single board, since nearly every part reads off that one dimension.

The top is doubled: a 1x3 strip glued to the front edge of a 1x8 board, both overhanging 1/2" on the sides and front, giving the top a thicker, more substantial front edge than a single board would. **This shelf must be screwed into wall studs**, not just anchored to drywall — hooks holding coats and bags put a real, repeated pull-out load on the mounting screws.

materials.name: Wood screws, 2-1/2", for hanging into studs
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Cut off at 45°, 4" back and 4" up from the bottom rear corner.
2-1/2" less than your desired overall shelf length.
2-1/2" less than your desired overall shelf length.
2-1/2" less than your desired overall shelf length.
Notched at top and back to pass through a 1x3; add or remove dividers to change the number of cubbies.

steps.title: Cut and assemble the frame
steps.body: Mark the sides with a speed square and cut off at a 45° angle, 4" back and 4" up from the bottom rear corner. Attach the top and back supports to the two sides with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Mark a square line at the top of the 1x6 bottom board across the side boards, where the bottom shelf sits. Attach the bottom shelf to both the bottom board and the sides with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Notch and install the dividers
steps.body: Notch out the dividers at the top and back to allow a 1x3 to pass through, cutting each while it lies flat before rotating it up into place. Attach the dividers inside the shelf with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top front trim to the underside of the top, flush to the back and overhanging 1/2" on the ends, with brad nails and glue. Glue the front edge of the top front trim and the assembled top, then attach the whole top assembly to the shelf, overhanging 1/2" on the sides and front.

steps.title: Finish and add hooks
steps.body: Fill nail holes with wood filler, sand with 120 grit, and paint or stain as desired. Install the wall hooks.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Hang the shelf
steps.body: Locate wall studs and attach the shelf to the studs with 2-1/2" wood screws. Touch up any exposed screw holes with paint.
materials: Wood screws, 2-1/2", for hanging into studs

unresolvedImages.alt: Wall-mounted entryway shelf with angled brackets, cubbies, and hooks
